content_body:
The happy days of summer are here, and tubing down the Toccoa River is a refreshing and exhilarating activity while staying in our luxury Blue Ridge, Georgia vacation rentals. Tubing in Blue Ridge, GA, and the surrounding area is a fun way to cool down, enjoy the beauty of Blue Ridge and the North Georgia mountains, and have a great time with friends and family.  

The Toccoa River is a dam-controlled river, and water is released daily, so be sure to stay alert or check the TVA website for water releases. Water levels and speed can change significantly after water is released. At low water, the river is shallow and moves at a slower pace, wonderful for leisurely tubing. At high water, the river rises 2 to 3 feet and picks up considerable speed. 

For Guests staying in Serendipity on Noontootla Creek, Cherokee Sunrise on Noontootla Creek, Above the Timberline, Aska Escape Lodge, and Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary, the following are convenient tubing outfitters:

The Toccoa Valley Campground operates a popular tubing operation during the summer months. Their trip is 6 miles long and lasts around 2-3 hours, depending on the water level. The tubes are custom-designed with a bottom and handle to make the trip more relaxing and pleasant, especially for children.  

11481 Aska Road
Blue Ridge, GA 30513
(706)838-4317

Van Zandt's Grill & Grocery/Tubing & Kayaking - 2 minutes from Serendipity on Noontootla Creek and Cherokee Sunrise on Noontootla Creek

103 NEWPORT ROAD 
Blue Ridge, GA 30513
(706) 838-4158

For Guests staying in Riverview Lodge, Fallen Timber Lodge, or The River's Edge, you can either bring your own tube and put it in at the property or go with Rolling Thunder River Company.

When tubing down the Toccoa River, you can enjoy the river in a number of ways. If you're staying at any of our Toccoa River cabin rentals, like Riverview Lodge, Fallen Timber Lodge, or The River's Edge, be sure to bring your own tube (or purchase at Walmart) and put in on the river. You will experience an awesome 2-hour float into Horseshoe Bend Park (provided Lake Blue Ridge Dam has not released water), which is outside of McCaysville. Have someone in your party pick you up, which is a short 15-minute drive from those cabins. This is one of our favorite summertime activities from your own cabin!

Rolling Thunder: They will shuttle you to the put-in, and you will float to the center where you parked your car from ages five and up. Floats can last from 20 minutes to 1.5 hours, depending on the water release from the dam at Lake Blue Ridge. They are open daily from 9 am to 5 pm.

Toccoa Tubing Center
20 Hughes Street
McCaysville, GA 30555
(800) 408-7238
title: Tubing on the Toccoa River
